fields.answer: As the first Business Operations & Strategy hire, you're looking for a generalist who can own pricing, strategic planning, and financial modeling while standing up new functions from scratch — which is exactly the work I've been doing. At M Science I led a cross-functional overhaul of our pricing and packaging model and stood up a pricing desk that drove a ~35% ARR increase in its first quarter, then built the financial and data infrastructure to make it repeatable. I'm drawn to Hang because it lets me do that end-to-end, working directly with the CEO on a consumer-brand platform I'd genuinely enjoy helping build.

Additional Information
fields.answer: I'm applying because Product Operations is the work I gravitate toward: owning the cross-functional problems that don't have a clean owner, then building the systems that make execution repeatable. At M Science I led a ground-up redesign of our packaging and pricing model, aligning data science, sales, and research and standing up a pricing desk, which drove a ~35% ARR increase the quarter after launch. I then built the infrastructure to make it stick, conceptualizing a Salesforce master-data system that unified our product metadata and automated customer entitlements. I do my own analysis in SQL and Python rather than waiting on a queue. OpenAI is shipping capability faster than most organizations can operationalize, and closing the gap between what the product can do and how a company actually runs on it is where I do my best work.

fields.question: I acknowledge that I have opened, read, and understood the Arbitration Agreement. I understand that by submitting my application, I am agreeing to be bound by the terms of the Arbitration Agreement.
I confirm I have read the above.
Additional Information
fields.answer: I've spent my career as a strategy and operations operator, running the analytical and system-level work behind revenue growth. At M Science I led a cross-functional overhaul of our pricing and packaging model, aligning sales, data science, and research and standing up a pricing desk, which drove a ~35% ARR increase the quarter after launch. I then built the infrastructure to make it durable: a Salesforce master data system for product metadata and entitlements, plus north-star metrics, Databricks pipelines, and executive dashboards. A central cadence role owning the forecasting and operating rhythm across GTM is exactly the kind of high-leverage, ambiguous problem I want to work on, and I'm self-sufficient in SQL and Python to do the analysis myself.

fields.question: I acknowledge that I have opened, read, and understood the Arbitration Agreement. I understand that by submitting my application, I am agreeing to be bound by the terms of the Arbitration Agreement.
I confirm I have read the above.
Additional Information
fields.answer: I build data-intensive products for financially sophisticated users, so this role sits right where I want to be. At M Science I own our KPI API, which forecasts critical financial metrics for hundreds of companies for institutional and quant investors. Through discovery I found the API's restatement structure was creating look-ahead bias that broke clients' models, then partnered with engineering on a point-in-time schema that fixed it, doubled the number of quant teams using the data, and grew product ARR by about 80%. I also built ML anomaly detection and LLM-based QA that cut our error rate from over 2% to near zero. Turning frontier AI into financial tooling that teams trust enough to make real decisions with is exactly the kind of problem I want to work on.

fields.question: I acknowledge that I have opened, read, and understood the Arbitration Agreement. I understand that by submitting my application, I am agreeing to be bound by the terms of the Arbitration Agreement.
I confirm I have read the above.

fields.answer: My most directly relevant experience is at PennyMac Loan Services, where I spent about a year and a half as a Data Analyst in mortgage lending. I was the Broker Direct data domain expert and senior point of contact, built the high-visibility SQL and Excel reporting leadership used to steer strategy across a large loan portfolio, and produced time-sensitive regulatory reports, so I've worked inside the compliance and data rigor a regulated lending business demands. Since then, at M Science I've spent three-plus years as a Product Manager building data products for institutional and quant investors, which keeps me fluent in financial-services data and the needs of a highly regulated customer base. That combination of hands-on mortgage-lending data experience and product ownership in financial services is why Mercury's business lending mandate resonates with me: I understand both how much the lending lifecycle depends on trustworthy data and decisioning, and how to turn that into products customers trust.

fields.question: Additional Information
fields.answer: I've spent my career shipping data-intensive products and building things from zero. At M Science I own our KPI API, where product discovery surfaced adoption blockers the quant teams hadn't framed as product problems; the schema we rebuilt with engineering nearly doubled the teams using the data and grew ARR by ~80%. I'm also a builder by instinct. At Audare AI I used prototyping tools like Bolt and Lovable to get concepts in front of users early instead of arguing them in a doc. ChatGPT Sites sits right at the intersection I care about: putting real AI capability into a consumer product people actually use to make something. That's the kind of problem I want to work on, and I'd bring hands-on discovery, a bias for building, and comfort operating at the lowest level of detail.

job_title: GTM Strategy & Operations Manager, Data & Territory Operations

fields.question: Yes but I would need to relocate
Yes but I would need to relocate
If yes to above, please elaborate
fields.answer: Most of my product experience has been building financial products. At M Science I own a data API that quantitative investors and hedge funds use to forecast company KPIs and drive trading decisions, which meant deep work on financial data integrity, point-in-time history, and how funds actually consume market data. Earlier, at PennyMac, I worked in mortgage lending as the data owner for the Broker Direct channel, building the reporting leadership used to steer strategy across a large loan portfolio. I have not worked directly with BaaS card-issuing partners like Stripe or Marqeta, but I have built and operated products squarely in regulated financial domains.

fields.question: Tell us a little bit about yourself and why you're a fit for this position.
fields.answer:
I am a product and strategy operator who has spent my career turning messy, data-heavy problems into products people trust to make real decisions with. At M Science I manage an API product that delivers forecasted KPIs for hundreds of public companies to quantitative investors. Through product discovery I found that the data structure was creating look-ahead bias that invalidated the product for quant funds, so I partnered with engineering to rebuild the schema around point-in-time history and worked with research to standardize the metadata. That overhaul grew the product's ARR by roughly 80% and doubled the number of quant teams using it. I also led a cross-functional pricing and packaging redesign that drove a 35% revenue increase, and I built the Salesforce-based master data system that automates entitlements and powers our unit economics.

What draws me to this Staff PM role is that Benepass sits exactly at the intersection where I work best: a fintech product where getting the money movement, partner integrations, and user experience right all matter at once. I have built financial products in regulated domains, I am self-sufficient in SQL and Python, and I am comfortable operating from strategy down to the lowest level of detail. I would bring that same generalist, first-principles approach to Benepass's fintech roadmap.

fields.question: Tell us a little bit about yourself and why you're a fit for this position.
fields.answer:
I am a generalist operator who has built my career on pulling signal out of noise, turning it into strategy, and then doing the operational work to execute it. At M Science I own product strategy and operations for a data API used by quantitative investors. I led a cross-functional overhaul of our pricing and packaging that drove a 35% revenue increase, and I architected a Salesforce-based master data system that automated a 40-plus-hour weekly manual entitlements process and became the semantic layer behind our customer portal, analytics, and unit economics. I am equally comfortable scoping strategy and rolling up my sleeves: when we automated entitlements, I wrote the Python mapping script to cut scope and then personally reviewed and corrected over 10,000 records to get the data clean enough to launch.

Product Operations is where I do my best work because it rewards exactly that blend of process design, cross-functional alignment, and technical self-sufficiency. Benepass's mission of making benefits actually usable is the kind of tangible, real-world problem I want to help scale, and I would bring rigor and an owner's mindset to the operational systems that let the product grow.

fields.question: Do you now, or will you in the future, require sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H-1B visa status, etc.) to work legally for our company in the United States? *
Are you based in the U.S?
What makes you excited about the ElevenLabs mission?
fields.answer: ElevenLabs is making voice a first-class interface and holding it to a quality bar that turns research breakthroughs into products people actually build on. That combination is what pulls me in. I've spent my career on data-intensive, technically hard products, taking emerging capability and turning it into something customers trust enough to rely on, and I want to do that for audio AI at the moment it's reshaping how people create and consume content. The accessibility side of the mission matters to me too; making any voice and any language available to anyone is exactly the kind of tangible, universal problem I've always wanted to work on. On the revenue strategy and operations side specifically, I'm drawn to building the pricing, packaging, and go-to-market infrastructure that lets a fast-moving research company scale its commercial engine without slowing the product down.

fields.question: Finally, tell us about a hard problem in your past!
fields.answer: The hardest I've ever worked was rebuilding our customer entitlements system at M Science. I inherited a manual process that ate 40-plus man-hours every week, and automating it meant first doing a brutal amount of manual data validation and transformation to populate the new system. My instinct as an operator is to figure out which work can be simplified or automated and which work simply has to be done. So I cut the scope every way I could, pulling in data science and engineering to find optimizations and personally writing a Python script to handle the complex mapping and shrink the number of records that needed human review. Once I'd exhausted every shortcut, I led a small team through two weeks of focused validation, and I personally reviewed and corrected over 10,000 records to get the data integrity we needed to launch. The automated system replaced the weekly manual grind entirely. It taught me that the unglamorous, detailed work is often exactly what makes a scalable system possible.

fields.question: I do not currently live in New York, San Francisco - but I am willing to relocate within 6 months
Why are you interested in working at Abridge?
fields.answer: Abridge sits at an intersection I care about a lot: genuinely hard AI applied to a problem that plainly matters. Turning messy clinical conversations into structured, trustworthy documentation is exactly the kind of data-intensive product problem I've built my career around, and doing it in healthcare means the payoff is real, less clinician burnout and more time with patients. I've spent my career as a generalist operator on technically sophisticated products, running discovery, turning insight into strategy, and then building the operational infrastructure to execute it. At M Science I led a cross-functional overhaul of our pricing and packaging that drove a ~35% ARR increase, and I built the master data system that became the semantic layer under our analytics and AI products. Product operations and strategy is where I do my best work, sitting between product, engineering, and go-to-market to make execution repeatable. I'd love to bring that to a company scaling AI in a space where getting it right actually improves people's lives.

We're a high-growth startup looking to work with ambitious people with a history of exceptional performance. Do you have one or two examples of exceptional performance you want to highlight?
fields.answer: Two that stand out, both from M Science. First, as a junior employee I was trusted to lead the cross-functional overhaul of our packaging and pricing model, working with data science, sales, and investment research to build a data-driven framework and stand up a pricing desk. ARR grew about 35% in the first quarter after rollout. Second, as owner of our KPI API, I found through customer discovery that the restatement structure created look-ahead bias for quant funds and that clients lacked the semantic context to use the data. I partnered with engineering on a new schema with point-in-time history, standardized dimension tables, and external IDs, then drove the GTM with sales and marketing leadership. That release doubled the number of quant teams using the product and grew its ARR by roughly 80%.
